description:
You are acting as a junior backup-verification engineer.  
Inside the home directory already exists a source folder that holds two business-critical text files:

1. /home/user/backup_src/file1.txt  
2. /home/user/backup_src/file2.txt  

Your job consists of three sub-steps that can be completed with roughly two or three terminal commands in total:

1. Create a gzip-compressed tar archive that contains **only** the two text files (no parent folders).  
   • Target archive path: /home/user/backup_archive/project_backup.tar.gz  

2. Unpack that archive to a separate restore location: /home/user/backup_restore  

3. Verify that every restored file is byte-for-byte identical to its original and write a small human-readable log.  
   • Log file path: /home/user/backup_log/integrity_check.log  
   • The log must contain one line per file, in the **exact** format:  
     file1.txt : MATCH  
     file2.txt : MATCH  
     (Each line ends with a single <newline>; there must be **no extra lines or spaces**.)

Success criteria that the automated checker will enforce:
• /home/user/backup_archive/project_backup.tar.gz exists and is a valid gzip-compressed tar archive.  
• Listing the archive (e.g. with `tar -tzf`) shows exactly `file1.txt` and `file2.txt` at its root—no directory prefixes.  
• /home/user/backup_restore/file1.txt and /home/user/backup_restore/file2.txt both exist after extraction.  
• The SHA-256 hash of every restored file is identical to the hash of its counterpart in /home/user/backup_src.  
• /home/user/backup_log/integrity_check.log exists, is a regular file, and contains precisely the two lines shown above, in that order, with no additional text.

Carry out the task using normal user privileges; you do not need sudo.  
Remember to create any intermediate directories that are missing.  
When everything is correct, the automated tests will pass.
truth:
Initial filesystem state BEFORE the agent starts:

Directory /home/user/backup_src exists and is readable/writable by the regular user.
It already contains two files:

1. /home/user/backup_src/file1.txt  
   Contents (exact, including final newline):  
   "Report id: FQ1-2024\nTotal revenue: $1,000,000\n"

2. /home/user/backup_src/file2.txt  
   Contents (exact, including final newline):  
   "HR internal directory v2.3\nLast updated: 2024-05-12\n"

The directories /home/user/backup_archive, /home/user/backup_restore, and /home/user/backup_log do NOT yet exist.

Expected state AFTER successful completion:

1. Archive  
   Path: /home/user/backup_archive/project_backup.tar.gz  
   • Must be a gzip-compressed tar archive.  
   • `tar -tzf /home/user/backup_archive/project_backup.tar.gz` outputs:  
     file1.txt  
     file2.txt

2. Restored files  
   • /home/user/backup_restore/file1.txt  → bytes identical to original file1.txt  
   • /home/user/backup_restore/file2.txt  → bytes identical to original file2.txt

3. Verification log  
   Path: /home/user/backup_log/integrity_check.log  
   Exact byte content (including newlines, no extra newline at EOF):  
   "file1.txt : MATCH\nfile2.txt : MATCH\n"

Automated tests will:

A. Confirm archive existence and correct internal path names.  
B. Extract archive to a temporary area and compare its files with the originals.  
C. Read /home/user/backup_log/integrity_check.log and assert it contains exactly the two required lines and nothing else.
